title: A733 datasheet and IDMAC burst recheck
question: Does the local A733 datasheet text or remaining model-suggested burst-mode lead reveal a new source-backed SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch patch?
findings: The A733 datasheet text is a high-level product/pin/electrical document, not a register manual. It confirms SMHC0 is SD3.0 and that SMC/SPC/TZMA are secure-only blocks, but does not provide SMHC0 IDMAC, bus-matrix, firewall, or master-permission register fields.
The local driver exposes SDXC_REG_DMAC and SDXC_IDMAC_FIX_BURST/IDMA_ON, but no separate A733 HBURST or IDMAC burst control field was found in the source.
The speculative single/no-fixed-burst lead was already closed: H017/H070 record no-fixed-burst evidence, and H095 records that the BSP uses FIX_BURST|IDMA_ON with no new SMHC0 burst-control register.
decision: No build and no patch. Do not repeat burst-mode or high-level datasheet searches without a real A733 register manual or a source tree naming a new SMHC0 IDMAC/bus-matrix field.
next_action: Continue waiting for maintainer/vendor feedback or mine firmware/source artifacts for a named SDMMC0 master/firewall/security handoff bit.
